notes: After exactly four consecutive valid_in cycles, valid_out must assert for one cycle and data_out must equal the sum of the four data_in values.
checks.pass_condition: valid_out is low before the fourth valid sample, goes high for exactly one cycle after the fourth sample, and data_out equals the accumulated sum of the four accepted data_in values.
checks.trigger_condition: When a deterministic stimulus drives four consecutive valid_in=1 samples.

notes: Boundary values (maximum 8‑bit data) must still produce a correct accumulated sum and a single-cycle valid_out.
checks.pass_condition: After four consecutive valid_in cycles with data_in=255, valid_out asserts for one cycle and data_out equals 4*255 = 1020 (fits in 10 bits).
checks.trigger_condition: When a deterministic stimulus drives four consecutive valid_in=1 samples with data_in set to the maximum 8‑bit value.

notes: Valid-low gaps must not be counted toward the four‑sample group; only cycles with valid_in=1 contribute to accumulation.
checks.pass_condition: When a stimulus interleaves valid_in=1 samples with valid_in=0 gaps, exactly four valid samples trigger a single valid_out pulse and data_out equals the sum of the four accepted data_in values.
checks.trigger_condition: When a deterministic stimulus drives four valid_in=1 samples separated by valid_in=0 cycles.

notes: A reset asserted mid‑group must clear any partial accumulation; only the post‑reset group may produce an output.
checks.pass_condition: If reset is asserted before a group of four valid samples completes, valid_out must remain low for the incomplete group and only assert after a later full group of four valid samples, with data_out reflecting the sum of the post‑reset samples.
checks.trigger_condition: When a deterministic stimulus drives a partial group, asserts reset, then drives a complete group.

notes: Two consecutive groups of four valid samples must each produce a distinct valid_out pulse and correct accumulated sum.
checks.pass_condition: After the first four valid samples, valid_out pulses once with the correct sum; after the next four valid samples, a second pulse occurs with the sum of the second group, with no extra pulses in between.
checks.trigger_condition: When a deterministic stimulus drives eight consecutive valid_in=1 samples (two groups of four).
